title: Disable CSV Export When Row Grouping Is Active
story: As a data analyst exporting metrics from the glossary, I want to be clearly informed when export is unavailable due to row grouping, so that I understand why my export contains unexpected data and can disable grouping to export correctly.
description:
Exporting metrics to CSV while row grouping is enabled produces incorrect results (e.g., only 1 record exported when many are visible). The DataTables library does not support export with row grouping enabled, as confirmed by their compatibility matrix.

The solution is to disable the export/copy buttons when grouping is active and show a popup alert explaining: 'Currently, export is disabled when grouping metrics.' This sets clear expectations rather than hiding the buttons entirely.
acceptance_criteria: Export and copy buttons are disabled when row grouping is active
A popup alert appears when a user attempts to export with grouping enabled
Alert message explains that export is disabled while grouping is active
Export works correctly when grouping is turned off
Export buttons re-enable when grouping is removed
ux_changes: Added a popup alert when users attempt to export, copy, or print while row grouping is enabled. Buttons remain visible but trigger the alert instead of the export action.
implementation_notes: DataTables RowGroup extension is incompatible with the Buttons export extension per the official compatibility matrix. A custom multi-sheet export approach was explored but deferred in favor of the simpler alert solution.
comment_summary: Developer found DataTables RowGroup is incompatible with export. Explored a multi-sheet Excel approach but it was deferred. Final solution: popup alert when export is attempted with grouping active. Ryan approved the alert approach.
